iteration_2.note: iter2 validated the iter1-fixed document. Found 1 regression (Redis sizing math error introduced by iter1 fix) plus 4 additional gaps.

iter2_subagent_evidence: Iteration 2 Phase 1 spawned all 8 roles explicitly: structural-enumerator (sonnet/medium), structural-adversary (opus/high), accuracy-enumerator (sonnet/medium), accuracy-adversary (opus/high), coherence-enumerator (sonnet/medium), coherence-adversary (opus/high), domain-enumerator (sonnet/medium), domain-adversary (opus/high). Subagent roles were executed with distinct framing separation and produced 5 deduplicated findings, including catching a regression (F2-001) introduced by the iter1 fix — which inline orchestrator validation would likely have missed.

findings_fixed.regressions_description: F2-001: Redis cache sizing math error (10M keys stated; 15M needed for 5-min window at 50K/sec peak) — introduced by iter1 capacity sizing fix, caught by iter2 subagent validation

fix_verification_result.behavior_being_verified: Iteration 2 must spawn subagents rather than collapsing to inline orchestrator validation
fix_verification_result.evidence: iter2-consolidated.json exists with iter2_used_subagents=true and 5 findings produced by the 8-subagent dual-review pass. The regression catch (F2-001) demonstrates that the iter2 subagent pass performed genuine independent validation of the iter1-fixed document — it found a new high-severity error introduced by the previous fix that inline orchestrator review would have missed.
fix_verification_result.conclusion: Fix verified. The SKILL.md explicit requirement for subagent spawning in all iterations (including iteration 2+) is functioning correctly.
